nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content:
## 2. Decide Whether a new Event Should Be Created
[Read more about using the Code]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

For more complex workflows, sometimes you may need to gather and prepare variables before the rest of the workflow runs. The Code node is one such tool to achieve this if you're familiar with code but its not the only way.

This workflow splits based on whether the event needs to be created. When it already exists, attendees are updated for the existing event instead.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note2

parameters.content:
## 3. Using AI Agent to Automate Event Creation
[Read more about AI Agents]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

AI Agents are generally described as self-governing LLMs with access to various "tools" which extend their knowledge and/or capability. In this demo, we've instructed the AI to do the following:
1. Generate a nice Event title.
2. Parse and assume dates and times from user message.
3. Parse the address and website of location/venue and to use the "tools" if it cannot get this data from the message or its own knowledge.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note3
nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note5
nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note6

parameters.content:
## Try It Out!
### This workflow does the following
* Monitors a slack channel for event messages tagged with the Calendar (📅) emoji.
* Checks if a calender event exists otherwise uses ChatGPT to create the calendar event; AI will add event title, description and location details.
* If calendar event exists, checks for channel users who have reacted with checkmark (✅) emoji.
* These users are automatically added as attendees to the event.

### Need Help?
Join the [Discord]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} or ask in the [Forum]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

Happy Hacking!

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Reply Invite with EventId
nodes.notes: This slack node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Calendar Event Booking Agent

parameters.text:
=Your role is to create professional event calendar events based on user message.
Given the following user message, do the following 5 tasks. Note, Assume all dates are within the current year which is {{ $now.format("yyyy") }} unless stated otherwise.
1. Summarize the message and generate a title for the event. Make sure it's eye-catching to attract more attendees!
2. Determine and extract the start date and time for the event. Date must be in the format yyyy-MM-dd and time in the format of hh:mm:ss. If no start time is indicated, the start time is 9am. If you are unable to do so, just leave it blank.
3. Determine and extract the end date and time for the event. Date must be in the format yyyy-MM-dd and time in the format of hh:mm:ss. If you are unable to do so, just leave it blank.
4. Where the user message refers to a location or venue, use the SerpAPI tool to search for this location or venue on the web and retrieve the full address.
5. Additionally, if the location or venue has a website or relevant webpage, return the URL of the location or venue.
6. Try to identify the event type as one of "social meeting", "social gathering", "business meeting", "business gathering" or "unknown".

user message:
```{{ $json.invite.json.text }}```

nodes.notes: This agent node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

parameters.text:
=Your role is to create professional event calendar events based on user message.
Given the following user message, do the following 5 tasks. Note, Assume all dates are within the current year which is {{ $now.format("yyyy") }} unless stated otherwise.
1. Summarize the message and generate a title for the event. Make sure it's eye-catching to attract more attendees!
2. Determine and extract the start date and time for the event. Date must be in the format yyyy-MM-dd and time in the format of hh:mm:ss. If no start time is indicated, the start time is 9am. If you are unable to do so, just leave it blank.
3. Determine and extract the end date and time for the event. Date must be in the format yyyy-MM-dd and time in the format of hh:mm:ss. If you are unable to do so, just leave it blank.
4. Where the user message refers to a location or venue, use the SerpAPI tool to search for this location or venue on the web and retrieve the full address.
5. Additionally, if the location or venue has a website or relevant webpage, return the URL of the location or venue.
6. Try to identify the event type as one of "social meeting", "social gathering", "business meeting", "business gathering" or "unknown".

user message:
```{{ $json.invite.json.text }}```

parameters.content:
## 2. Decide Whether a new Event Should Be Created
[Read more about using the Code]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

For more complex workflows, sometimes you may need to gather and prepare variables before the rest of the workflow runs. The Code node is one such tool to achieve this if you're familiar with code but its not the only way.

This workflow splits based on whether the event needs to be created. When it already exists, attendees are updated for the existing event instead.

🚨**Required**
* Set the Google Calendar to use here.

## 3. Using AI Agent to Automate Event Creation
[Read more about AI Agents]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

AI Agents are generally described as self-governing LLMs with access to various "tools" which extend their knowledge and/or capability. In this demo, we've instructed the AI to do the following:
1. Generate a nice Event title.
2. Parse and assume dates and times from user message.
3. Parse the address and website of location/venue and to use the "tools" if it cannot get this data from the message or its own knowledge.

### Part 1: Creating the Event
This branch runs when we have a new Event.

## Try It Out!
### This workflow does the following
* Monitors a slack channel for event messages tagged with the Calendar (📅) emoji.
* Checks if a calender event exists otherwise uses ChatGPT to create the calendar event; AI will add event title, description and location details.
* If calendar event exists, checks for channel users who have reacted with checkmark (✅) emoji.
* These users are automatically added as attendees to the event.

### Need Help?
Join the [Discord]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} or ask in the [Forum]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

Happy Hacking!

### Part 2: Adding Attendees to Event
This branch runs if the event already exists.

🚨**Required**
* Set the channel to monitor here. Also, make sure to use the user access token in your credential

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: No custom credentials required for utility-only operations.
configurationSteps: Open the scheduler node "Schedule Trigger".
Adjust the execution intervals (e.g., Every hour, Daily, or Custom CRON expression) according to your preferences.
Open the "For Each User ID..." node (n8n-nodes-base.splitOut) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get User" Slack node.
Select the Channel or User ID where message notifications should be delivered.
Open the "Search for Invite Requests" Slack node.
Select the Channel or User ID where message notifications should be delivered.

Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Get Old EventId".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.

testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Your Slack channel will receive customized real-time notifications when the automation executes.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
